class.name: HEGoAssetNode
class.brief_description: Represents an instantiated Houdini Digital Asset (HDA) node.
class.description:
Use this class to instantiate an HDA by operator name, set parameters, cook, and fetch output data.
Call [method HEGoBaseNode.instantiate] after assigning [member op_name]. Most data-access methods require a valid instantiated node; when called before instantiation they log an error and return a failed task.
All methods that interact with Houdini Engine return an [HEGoTask]. Await the task to get the result: [codeblock] var task = asset_node.cook() var result = await _await_task(task) [/codeblock]
[method fetch_points] expects a resource compatible with [code]HEGoFetchPointsConfig[/code]. [method fetch_surfaces] expects a resource compatible with [code]HEGoFetchSurfacesConfig[/code].

method.name: cook
method.description:
Submits a cook task for this asset node.
Returns an [HEGoTask] that completes when cooking is finished. The task result is [code]null[/code].

method.name: fetch_heightfield_layer_image
method.description:
Submits a task to fetch one heightfield layer as an [Image] from the specified part id.
The task result is an [Image], or an invalid image reference when the node is not instantiated or the requested part cannot be read.

method.name: fetch_points
method.description:
Submits a task to fetch point data using a point-fetch configuration resource.
Config fields are interpreted as follows: [code]filter_attribs[/code] and [code]filter_attrib_values[/code]: paired by index; a point is kept only if all [code]point_attr == filter_value[/code] checks pass. [code]filter_attribs[/code] and [code]filter_attrib_values[/code] should have the same length. [code]read_attribs[/code]: extra point attributes to fetch at leaf level (in addition to [code]P[/code], which is always included). [code]split_attribs[/code]: point attributes used to build nested dictionary groups.
The method reads from the display mesh part of the cooked node.
The task result is a [Dictionary] with the following structure: If [code]split_attribs[/code] is empty, the result is a flat dictionary with leaf keys. If [code]split_attribs[/code] is non-empty, each split attribute adds one nesting level keyed by that attribute's value. Leaf dictionaries contain: [code]ids[/code]: filtered point indices in the source point array. [code]P[/code]: [Array] of [Vector3] positions. Additional keys from [code]read_attribs[/code], each mapped to an [Array] aligned with [code]ids[/code].
Returns an empty dictionary when no points pass filters, when no mesh part exists, or when geometry query fails.

method.name: fetch_surfaces
method.description:
Submits a task to fetch surface data using a surface-fetch configuration resource.
Config fields are interpreted as follows: [code]normal[/code], [code]color[/code], [code]uv[/code], [code]uv2[/code], [code]tangents[/code]: toggles for point-derived channels included in generated Godot surface arrays. [code]read_attribs[/code], [code]filter_attribs[/code], [code]filter_attrib_values[/code], [code]split_attribs[/code]: primitive-attribute lists used for metadata readback, filtering, and grouping. [code]filter_attribs[/code] and [code]filter_attrib_values[/code] are paired by index. [code]filter_attribs[/code] and [code]filter_attrib_values[/code] should have the same length.
The method reads the display mesh part.
The task result is a [Dictionary] with the following structure: If [code]split_attribs[/code] is empty, the root is a single leaf dictionary. If [code]split_attribs[/code] is non-empty, result is nested by split attribute values. Each leaf contains: [code]surface_array[/code]: a Godot [code]Mesh.ARRAY_MAX[/code]-sized array usable with [code]ArrayMesh.add_surface_from_arrays()[/code], including at minimum vertices and indices; optional channels are present depending on config and source attributes. [code]ids[/code]: filtered primitive ids local to the current filtered set. Any primitive attributes requested through [code]read_attribs[/code], each as an [Array] aligned with [code]ids[/code].
Returns an empty dictionary when no mesh part exists, no faces are available, no elements pass filters, or when geometry query fails.

method.name: get_heightfield_layers
method.description:
Submits a task to return available heightfield layers from the cooked asset.
Use [code]read_prim_attribs[/code] to request primitive attributes that should be included in each returned layer entry.
The task result is an [Array] where each entry is a [Dictionary] containing layer metadata such as part id, layer name, voxel dimensions, scale, and transform information.

method.name: get_input_names
method.description:
Submits a task to return the input names defined on this HDA node.
The task result is a [PackedStringArray] of input names. This is useful for mapping script-side inputs to operator input slots.

method.name: get_parms_dict
method.description:
Submits a task to return a dictionary describing the current parameter values.
The task result is a [Dictionary] intended for inspection and tooling workflows, mirroring parameter state read from HAPI.

method.name: get_preset
method.description:
Submits a task to serialize current parameter state as a Houdini preset blob.
The task result is a [PackedByteArray]. Use together with [method set_preset] to save and restore HDA parameter setups.

method.name: insert_multiparm_instance
method.description:
Submits a task to insert one multiparm instance at [code]index[/code] for the parameter id.
Use this to grow multiparm blocks before setting values on newly created entries.

method.name: instantiate
method.description:
Submits a task to instantiate the HDA node from [member op_name].
If [member op_name] is empty, the task fails with an error.
On success, the created node id is stored and this wrapper is registered in the session manager.

method.name: remove_multiparm_instance
method.description:
Submits a task to remove one multiparm instance at [code]index[/code] for the parameter id.
Indices follow Houdini multiparm ordering.

method.name: set_parm
method.description:
Submits a task to set a parameter by name.
The [Variant] value type is converted to the matching Houdini parameter storage type when possible.
Call after [method HEGoBaseNode.instantiate]. Invalid node state causes the task to fail.

method.name: set_parms_batch
method.description:
Submits a single task to set multiple parameters at once.
[code]parms[/code] is a [Dictionary] mapping parameter names ([String]) to values ([Variant]). All parameters are set in one HAPI round-trip, which is more efficient than calling [method set_parm] repeatedly.

method.name: set_preset
method.description:
Submits a task to apply a serialized Houdini parameter preset to this asset.
The preset payload should come from [method get_preset] or a compatible Houdini preset source.

members.member: Houdini operator name used by [method HEGoBaseNode.instantiate] to create the asset node.
